# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:  # Define your stages in GitLab CI/CD pipeline (Required)
    - Install Dependencies
    - Testing
    - Build
    - Deploy
variables:   # Variable to store environment specific information for the project. Required by all jobs within a stage and must be set before any job runs on that particular run of GitLab CI/CD pipeline (Required) –> e.g., $CI_PIPELINE_ID,$CI_PROJECT_*
    - PYTHON_VERSION: '3' # Python version to use for the project requirements file and virtual environment creation or where pip install is executed from here onwards as per requirement(Defaults are set in .gitlab-ci.yml) (Optional but recommended if you have any custom setup).
    - COMMIT_MESSAGE: 'chore(release): prepare for new release [skip ci]' # Default commit message when a change is committed, can be modified to suit your project requirements –> e.g., "refs/heads/*" (Optional) If you want the default changeset info and no other details in gitlab-ci yml file then use '[skip ci].'.
pipelines:   # Define pipelines for each stage of GitLab CI/CD pipeline –> e.g., Install Dependencies, Testing etc (Required) - Each job within a specific run or commit must be executed on that particular step in the gitlab-ci file defined here and it should follow this order
    install_dependencies: # Identifier for each stage to define jobs under them –> e.g., Install Dependency Jobs, Testing etc (Required) - Each job within a specific run or commit must be executed on that particular step in the gitlab-ci file defined here and it should follow this order
        image: python:$PYTHON_VERSION # GitLab CI/CD container to use for each stage. Required when you have multiple stages (Optional) - e.g., 'python' or any other specific version of Python like 3,2 etc(Required). If not specified then it uses the default one from .gitlab-ci.yml file if exists otherwise python:latest
        script: # Define your scripts for each job to be executed on that particular step in GitLab CI/CD pipeline (Optional but recommended) - e.g., pip install --upgrade, pytest etc(Required). If not defined then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise no script is provided here.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to be executed on a step in GitLab CI/CD pipeline.)
        only: # Define which branches or tags should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., master, /^feature\/.*$/, ^refs/tags/.*/.* $ refs/heads/* –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none.
        environment: # Define which environments should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., production, staging etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to run in different envs.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        tags: # Define which labels should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., my_label, ^myLabel$ etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs with same label to run in GitLab CI/CD pipeline.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none.
        artifacts: # Define which directories or files should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., coverage, logs etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to collect data in different ways.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        cache: # Define which directories or files should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., pip, pytest etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to collect data in different ways.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        before_script: # Define scripts that should run once, regardless of job status (Optional) - e.g., set up virtualenv and install dependencies etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to setup data like environment.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        after_script: # Define scripts that should run once, regardless of job status (Optional) - e.g., test coverage etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to collect data in different ways.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        allow_failure: # Define whether a job should fail or succeed, regardless of result (Optional) - e.g., true/false etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to handle different scenarios.) –> Default is false but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        stage: # Define which stages should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., Install Dependencies, Testing etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to run in different steps.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        environment: # Define which environments should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., production, staging etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to run in different envs.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
        tags: # Define which labels should trigger this job (Optional) - e.g., my_label, ^myLabel$ etc.(Recommended when you have multiple jobs to run in same label.) –> Default is all but can be defined as per requirement(Required). If not specified then it will use the default one from .gitlab-ci file if exists otherwise none
